rationale: Determinism check on receive_impl is unprovable from the available specs. Two structural blockers, confirmed empirically: (1) The abstract receive_ack spec uses `Self{ send_state: ..., ..post }` (not `..pre`), leaving post.receive_state unconstrained at the view level when ack_seqno > num_packets_acked; consequence: `assert(post1@.receive_state =~= post2@.receive_state)` fails for the Ack/new-ack subcase. (2) The determinism equality compares concrete `HashMap<V>.epmap` fields; HashMap is external_body with an `uninterp spec fn view`, and no view->concrete equality axiom exists, so even when view equality holds, `assert(post1.receive_state.epmap == post2.receive_state.epmap)` fails. I verified empirically that the abstract send_state IS determined (`post1@.send_state =~= post2@.send_state` passes) but receive_state is not. Closing the gap would require introducing axioms forbidden by policy (assume / external_body). Prior attempt logged in the project tree reached the identical residua
